title: The One With The Metaphorical Tunnel

expressions.expression: There's gotta be a better way!
expressions.dialogue:
Host: these darn cartons are so flingin'-flangin' hard to open.
Joey: (rips open the carton and spills milk) Aw! There's got to be a better way!
Mike: And there is Kevin.
expressions.speaker: Joey (TV commercial character 'Kevin')
expressions.meaning: 더 나은 방법이 있어야 한다! — 현재 방식에 불만을 표하며 해결책을 원하는 표현
expressions.nuance: 미국 홈쇼핑(infomercial)의 클리셰 문구로 널리 패러디됨. 'have got to'→'gotta'로 줄이는 전형적인 구어 축약. 일상에서도 짜증스러운 상황을 과장하며 쓸 수 있음.
expressions.example: I keep losing my keys every single morning. There's gotta be a better way! → 매일 아침 열쇠를 잃어버려. 이것보다 더 나은 방법이 있어야 해!

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Oh, you said it Mike. (Rips open the carton and spills milk on the counter) Aw! There's got to be a better way!

expressions.dialogue:
Host: these darn cartons are so flingin'-flangin' hard to open.
...
Chandler: You're flingin'-flangin' right I am!
expressions.speaker: Infomercial Host / Chandler
expressions.meaning: 욕설(f***ing)을 방송용으로 순화한 완곡 표현. 강한 짜증이나 흥분을 나타냄.
expressions.nuance: 방송이나 아이 앞처럼 욕을 참아야 할 때 쓰는 대체 표현. 'darn', 'freakin'', 'friggin'' 등도 같은 역할. 과장된 어감이 유머 효과를 낳기도 함.
expressions.example: This flingin'-flangin' printer jams every time I have a deadline! → 이 빌어먹을 프린터는 마감 때마다 꼭 걸린다니까!

occ.speaker: Host
occ.text: Folks, has this ever happened to you. You go to the refrigerator to get a nice glass of milk, (Joey is in the background struggling to open a cartoon of milk) and these darn cartons are so flingin'-flangin' hard to open.

expressions.dialogue:
Joey: My agent would like to know why I didn't show up at the audition I didn't know I had today. The first good thing she gets me in weeks. How could you not give me the message?!
Chandler: Well, I'll tell ya I do enjoy guilt, but, ah, it wasn't me.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 어떻게 그럴 수가 있어?! — 상대의 행동에 강한 비난과 실망을 담은 수사의문문
expressions.nuance: 'Why didn't you ~?'보다 감정이 훨씬 강하게 실려 있음. 실제로 답을 원하는 게 아니라 분노·상처를 표현. 'How could you do this to me?' 형태로도 폭넓게 활용.
expressions.example: How could you not tell me the party was cancelled? I showed up in a suit! → 파티 취소됐다는 걸 어떻게 말 안 할 수가 있어? 나 정장까지 입고 갔잖아!

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: How could you not get it? You were Santa last year.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Ethan, focus. How could you not tell me?

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: I can't believe you married Duncan. I mean how could you not tell me? We lived together, we told each other everything.

expressions.dialogue:
Joey: How could you not give me the message?!
Chandler: Well, I'll tell ya I do enjoy guilt, but, ah, it wasn't me.
Phoebe: Yes, it was! It was him! (then) Okay, it was me!
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 죄책감을 즐기는 편이긴 한데, 이번엔 내 잘못이 아니야 — 역설적 유머로 결백을 주장하는 표현
expressions.nuance: 'I do + 동사' 구조는 강조를 위해 조동사 do를 쓰는 패턴. 여기서는 '죄책감을 즐긴다'는 역설적 표현에 do가 결합되어 Chandler 특유의 자기비하 유머를 만들어냄.
expressions.example: Oh, I do love being blamed for things I didn't do — but check the security footage this time. → 내가 안 한 일로 비난받는 거 정말 좋아하긴 하는데 — 이번엔 CCTV 확인해봐.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Uhh, hey look, I don't really enjoy being with other men that way. But, um, zoo dollars?

expressions.dialogue:
Phoebe: Can I use the phone again?
Rachel: Sure Pheebs, you know, that's what it's there for, emergencies and pretend agents.
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.meaning: 그게 바로 그것이 있는 이유야 — 어떤 것의 존재 목적을 설명하거나 가볍게 허락할 때 쓰는 표현
expressions.nuance: 친절하게 허락할 때('물론, 그러라고 있는 거잖아')도, 살짝 빈정댈 때도 쓰임. Rachel은 'pretend agents'를 붙여 Phoebe를 놀리면서도 허락. 'That's what friends are for'와 같은 패턴.
expressions.example: 'Can I borrow your charger?' 'Go ahead, that's what it's there for.' → '충전기 빌려도 돼?' '써, 빌리라고 있는 거잖아.'

occ.speaker: Mr. Geller
occ.text: There you go. So she dips into her savings, that's what it's there for. She's gonna be fine, and if you need a little extra, you know where to find it. [pulls a quarter from behind her ear]

expressions.dialogue:
Joey: Well, Estelle tried, you know. The casting director told her that I missed my chance.
Phoebe: That is unfair. I'll call her and tell her it was totally my fault.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 기회를 놓치다 — 타이밍을 잘못 잡아 기회를 잃었을 때 쓰는 표현
expressions.nuance: 'lose a chance'보다 더 자연스럽고 흔한 표현. 'blow my chance'는 자기 실수 강조, 'miss my chance'는 타이밍의 문제에 더 가까움. 취업·연애·오디션 등 모든 상황에 적용.
expressions.example: I was going to invest in that startup years ago but hesitated. I really missed my chance. → 몇 년 전에 그 스타트업에 투자하려다 망설였어. 진짜 기회를 놓쳤어.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: No! Because he's in love with the British chippy! Look, Rachel, if you go, you're just gonna mess with his head and ruin his wedding! Y'know, it's too late! You missed you're chance! I'm sorry, I know this must be really hard, it's over.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: What can I say, you missed your chance. From now on the only person who's going to enjoy these bad boys (holds up his hands) is me. (Quickly realizes what he said and exits disgustedly.)

occ.speaker: Kori
occ.text: I thought so many times about calling him and asking him out. I guess I really missed my chance.

expressions.expression: What a sad little life she must lead.
expressions.dialogue:
Joey: Phoebe, you can't do that. The casting director doesn't talk to friends, she only talks to agents.
Phoebe: What a sad little life she must lead. Okay, ooh (starts dialing).
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.meaning: 얼마나 불쌍한 삶을 살고 있을까 — 상대방의 좁은 태도를 동정하는 척 비꼬는 표현
expressions.nuance: 'What a ~' 감탄문 구조. 직접 비판하지 않고 동정하는 척하며 상대를 가볍게 깎아내리는 어감. Phoebe 특유의 순진한 듯 뼈 있는 화법.
expressions.example: 'He refuses to try any food that isn't Korean.' 'What a sad little life he must lead.' → '그는 한국 음식 아니면 아무것도 안 먹대.' '얼마나 불쌍한 삶이야.'

expressions.dialogue: Joey: it seems to me it's pretty much like anything else, you know, face your fear. If you have a fear of heights, you go to the top of the building! If you're afraid of bugs... get a bug. In this case, you have a fear of commitment, so I say you go in there and be the most committed guy there ever was.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 두려움을 정면으로 직면하라 — 무서운 것을 피하지 않고 맞서는 것이 해결책이라는 조언
expressions.nuance: 심리 치료·자기계발에서 흔히 쓰이는 표현. 'confront your fear'와 비슷. Joey의 단순하지만 사실 꽤 효과적인 논리로 전달된다는 점이 이 장면의 개그 포인트.
expressions.example: 'I've been avoiding driving since my accident.' 'You just have to face your fear and get back behind the wheel.' → '사고 이후 운전을 피해왔어.' '그냥 두려움을 직면하고 다시 운전대를 잡아야 해.'

expressions.dialogue:
Chandler: You think?
Joey: Oh, yeah. Go for it man, jump off the high dive, stare down the barrel of the gun, pee into the wind!
Chandler: Yeah, Joe, I assure you if I'm staring down the barrel of a gun, I'm pretty much peeing every which way.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 도전해봐! / 한번 해봐! — 강하게 격려하며 시도를 권유하는 표현
expressions.nuance: 매우 구어적이고 캐주얼한 표현. 'try it'보다 훨씬 더 열정적이고 적극적인 뉘앙스. 연애·스포츠·직업 등 모든 도전 상황에 사용 가능하며 보통 짧게 단독으로 씀.
expressions.example: 'I've been thinking about asking for a raise.' 'Go for it! You've been working overtime every week.' → '연봉 인상 요청할까 생각 중이야.' '해봐! 매주 야근하잖아.'

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Okay, relax, relax. We are gonna be here for a while, it looks like, and we still have boyfriends and your career to cover.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Hey, look, you guys, I'm going for anything here, OK? I cannot be a waitress anymore, I mean it. I'm sick of the lousy tips, I'm sick of being called 'Excuse me...'

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: (Tearfully) Ross, I'm doing everything that I can, I've got everybody looking for him, and I- (Door buzzer goes and she runs to get it) Oh! Who is it?

expressions.dialogue:
Joey: Go for it man, jump off the high dive, stare down the barrel of the gun, pee into the wind!
Chandler: Yeah, Joe, I assure you if I'm staring down the barrel of a gun, I'm pretty much peeing every which way.
expressions.speaker: Joey / Chandler
expressions.meaning: 원래는 역효과 나는 무모한 행동의 비유(바람 향해 소변 보면 자기한테 튄다). Joey는 반대로 용기의 예시로 잘못 사용.
expressions.nuance: 원뜻은 '헛수고를 하다 / 스스로 화를 자초하다'. Joey의 의도와 달리 쓰인다는 점이 개그 포인트. Chandler가 'peeing every which way'로 원래 뜻을 살려 받아치는 언어 개그.
expressions.example: Trying to fight city hall over this permit is like peeing into the wind — you'll never win. → 허가 문제로 시청에 맞서는 건 바람 향해 오줌 누는 격이야 — 절대 못 이겨.

expressions.dialogue: Phoebe: (in 'Katelynn's' voice) 'Okay, well, um, she's in her car I'll have to patch you through.' (switching to normal voice) 'Okay, go ahead.' Hi Annie.
expressions.speaker: Phoebe (as Katelynn)
expressions.meaning: 전화를 다른 사람/선으로 연결해 드리다
expressions.nuance: 비즈니스 전화 응대에서 자주 쓰이는 공식 표현. 'transfer your call'과 비슷하지만 특히 무선·위성·비서 경유 연결 시 'patch'를 씀. 지금도 콜센터·비서 상황에서 들을 수 있는 표현.
expressions.example: Please hold — I'll patch you through to the billing department right away. → 잠시만요 — 청구 부서로 바로 연결해 드리겠습니다.

expressions.dialogue: Phoebe: (on phone as Katelynn) 'Will he work for scale?' you ask me. Well, I don't know about that, (Joey clears his throat to signify yes) except that I do and he will.
expressions.speaker: Phoebe (as Katelynn)
expressions.meaning: 배우·음악가 등이 조합(SAG-AFTRA 등) 규정 최저 임금으로 일하다 — 큰 보수 없이 기본 임금만 받고 일하는 것
expressions.nuance: 미국 연예인 노조의 최저 규정 임금을 'scale'이라 함. 신인 배우가 경력을 쌓기 위해 받아들이는 조건. 비연예계에서는 '최저 시급으로 일하다'는 의미로 확장되기도 함.
expressions.example: The director loved him but the studio only had budget for scale — he took it anyway to build his resume. → 감독은 그를 마음에 들어 했지만 스튜디오 예산이 기본급밖에 안 됐어 — 경력을 위해 수락했대.

expressions.dialogue:
Rachel: If you feel yourself reaching for that phone, then you go shoe shopping, you get your butt in a bubble bath. You want her back you have to start acting aloof.
Monica: She has to know that you're not ready.
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.meaning: 관심 없는 척하다 / 쿨하게 거리를 두다 — 상대방에게 진심을 숨기고 여유 있어 보이는 연애 전략
expressions.nuance: 'aloof' = 거리를 두는, 냉담한. 연애에서 'playing hard to get'과 비슷한 심리 전략. 너무 적극적으로 보이지 않아야 상대가 더 관심 가진다는 논리. 부정적 뜻도 있으므로 맥락 파악 중요.
expressions.example: She's been texting you first every day — stop replying instantly, act a little aloof. → 그 여자가 매일 먼저 문자 보내잖아 — 즉시 답장 그만 하고 좀 쿨하게 굴어봐.

expressions.expression: accidentally run into someone on purpose
expressions.dialogue:
Rachel: So, what you have to do is, you have to accidentally run into her on purpose. And then act aloof.
Chandler: So I'm not gonna lose her?
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.meaning: 의도적으로 우연한 만남을 꾸미다 — 계획한 만남이지만 마치 우연처럼 보이게 하는 행동
expressions.nuance: 'accidentally'(우연히)와 'on purpose'(의도적으로)는 원래 반의어인데 함께 써서 연애 전략의 아이러니를 유머 있게 표현. 이런 모순적 표현이 영어 구어에서 꽤 자연스럽게 쓰임.
expressions.example: I 'accidentally' ran into him on purpose at his gym, but he saw me walk in three different times. → '우연히' 그의 헬스장에서 마주치려 했는데, 그가 내가 세 번이나 들어오는 걸 봤어.

expressions.dialogue:
Monica: You know, when you start getting screwed over all the time, you gotta switch to low-fat.
Rachel: Yeah, you do.
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: 당하다 / 부당하게 이용당하거나 피해를 입다
expressions.nuance: 다소 직설적인 구어 표현. 'get cheated'나 'get taken advantage of'보다 강도가 세고 감정적임. 연애·직장·거래 등 다양한 상황에 사용. 여기서는 Monica와 Rachel이 반복적으로 연애에서 상처받는 상황을 가리킴.
expressions.example: He got screwed over by his business partner — the guy took all the money and vanished. → 그는 사업 파트너한테 제대로 당했어 — 그 사람이 돈 다 들고 사라졌어.

expressions.dialogue:
Monica: Well, no, not at all, you're not terminal, you just need some damage control.
Chandler: Okay, so should I call her?
Rachel and Monica: Nooo!
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: 피해 수습 — 이미 잘못된 상황을 더 나빠지지 않도록 관리하는 것
expressions.nuance: 원래 군사·정치 용어였으나 연애·비즈니스·일상 전반에서 폭넓게 쓰임. 'I need to do damage control'처럼 동사구로도 자주 씀. 상황을 완전히 해결하는 게 아니라 '최악을 막는다'는 뉘앙스.
expressions.example: I accidentally liked my ex's photo from three years ago — full damage control: unlike, close the app, cry. → 실수로 전 남자친구 3년 전 사진에 좋아요를 눌렀어 — 완전 수습 모드: 취소, 앱 닫기, 울기.

occ.speaker: Estelle
occ.text: All right, first thing we gotta do, damage control.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Monica, you have to do some damage control here, okay. 'Cause he's feeling like... (the door opens and Chandler walks in with a pizza)

expressions.dialogue:
Chandler: And then I just, you know, threw the bag of barley at her, and ran out of the store.
Monica: My God! Chandler, we said be 'aloof' not 'a doof'.
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: 쿨하게 굴어, 바보같이 굴지 말고 — Chandler의 실패를 운율로 비꼬는 표현. 'doof' = doofus(바보)의 축약
expressions.nuance: 'aloof'와 'a doof'의 라임(운율)을 활용한 언어유희. 영어에서 이런 rhyming criticism은 간결하고 인상적이어서 농담이나 비판에 자주 등장. 'doofus' = 어수룩하고 멍청한 사람.
expressions.example: 'You waved at her from across the restaurant instead of playing it cool?' 'Yeah, I tried to be aloof and ended up being a total doof.' → '쿨한 척하려다 레스토랑 반대편에서 손 흔들었다고?' '응, 쿨하게 굴려다 완전 바보가 됐어.'

expressions.dialogue:
Chandler: I've scared ya'! I've said too much! I'm hopeless, and awkward, and desperate for love!!
(Janice leaves. Chandler calls her answering machine.)
Chandler: Hey, Janice! It's me. Um, I just wanna apologize in advance for having chased you down the street.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 내가 너무 많이 말했어 — 실수로 지나치게 솔직하거나 과도하게 털어놓았을 때 하는 말
expressions.nuance: 후회와 당혹감이 담긴 표현. 비밀을 누설했을 때보다 감정 표현이 지나쳤을 때 더 자주 씀. 가볍게 웃으며 쓰기도 하고 Chandler처럼 절박하게 쓰기도 함.
expressions.example: I told my date everything about my ex on the very first date. I've said too much. → 첫 데이트에서 전 남자친구 얘기를 다 털어놨어. 말이 너무 많았어.

expressions.dialogue:
Chandler: So, you don't think I'm terminal?
Monica: Well, no, not at all, you're not terminal, you just need some damage control.
expressions.speaker: Monica / Chandler
expressions.meaning: (관계가) 돌이킬 수 없을 정도로 망한 건 아니야 — 의학 용어 '말기'를 연애 상황에 비유한 표현
expressions.nuance: Rachel이 '말기 환자용 아이스크림'으로 시작한 메타포를 이어받아 씀. 'terminal' = 말기 환자처럼 희망 없는 상태. 심각한 상황을 블랙 유머로 승화한 Friends 특유의 표현.
expressions.example: 'I replied 'you too' when the waiter said enjoy your meal. Is this friendship terminal?' 'No, everyone does that.' → '웨이터가 맛있게 드세요 했는데 '당신도요' 해버렸어. 이 관계 끝난 거야?' '아니, 다들 해.'

expressions.dialogue:
Joey: Wait a minute, did you just make up all that stuff just to get out of being my agent?
Phoebe: (in a coy tone) Oh, you caught me. I am so busted.
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.meaning: 완전히 들켰다 / 딱 걸렸다 — 비밀이나 꾀가 발각되었을 때 하는 말
expressions.nuance: 'busted' = 발각된 (경찰 은어에서 일상어로 확장). 'I got caught'보다 더 구어적이고 자기 자신을 가볍게 표현. 보통 큰 죄의식보다 당혹감이나 재미있다는 뉘앙스가 섞임.
expressions.example: 'Were you reading my diary?' 'I am so busted. I'm sorry, I was just curious.' → '내 일기 읽었어?' '완전 들켰다. 미안해, 그냥 궁금했어.'

expressions.dialogue:
Joey: you're a better agent than she is, but at least with her I don't want to blow my brains out.
...agents always lie. You know, Estelle just says stuff like 'They went another way', but this, I can use this.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 다른 방향으로 결정했다 — 실제 이유 없이 거절을 완곡하게 표현하는 연예·비즈니스 업계 클리셰
expressions.nuance: 'We've decided to go in a different direction'의 축약형. 실제 피드백 없이 부드럽게 거절할 때 씀. Joey는 이런 완곡어법보다 Phoebe의 직접적인 피드백이 더 유용하다고 말함.
expressions.example: 'How did the audition go?' 'They said they went another way — I'll never know what I did wrong.' → '오디션 어떻게 됐어?' '다른 방향으로 갔다고 하더라 — 내가 뭘 잘못했는지 영원히 모르겠지.'

expressions.dialogue:
Phoebe: Um, the off-Broadway play people said 'You were pretty but dumb.'
Joey: Oh.
Phoebe: Oh no wait, I'm sorry, that's 'pretty dumb.'
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.meaning: 'pretty'가 형용사(예쁜)가 아닌 부사(꽤, 상당히)로 쓰인 것. Phoebe가 처음에 'pretty but dumb(예쁘지만 멍청하다)'으로 잘못 전달하는 것이 개그.
expressions.nuance: 'pretty' = quite / fairly / somewhat (부사). 'pretty good', 'pretty bad', 'pretty sure' 등 일상에서 매우 자주 쓰임. 한국인이 '예쁜'으로만 알고 있어 부사 용법을 놓치기 쉬운 단어.
expressions.example: 'How was the negotiation?' 'Pretty tough, but I'm pretty sure we got a good deal.' → '협상 어땠어?' '꽤 힘들었는데, 좋은 조건 얻은 것 같아.'

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Well, ah, I'm an actor. I'm fairly neat. I ah, I got my own TV. Oh, and don't worry I'm totally okay with the gay thing.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Are you, are you, are you sure it's ah, a new bump? I mean, no offense, I've always thought of Ben as a fairly bumpy headed child.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Yeah, no, I'm fairly intuitive and psychic. It's a substantial gift.

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: Hello, Janice. Hi, I'm so glad that you called, I know I've been acting really weird lately. And, it's just because I'm crazy about you, and I just got...stupid, and, and scared, and....stupid a couple of more times. I'm sorry.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 당신에게 완전히 빠져있어 / 너를 너무 좋아해 — 강렬한 감정적 끌림을 나타내는 표현
expressions.nuance: 'I love you'보다 가볍지만 'I like you'보다 강한 표현. 연인 사이뿐 아니라 취미·음식에도 쓸 수 있음('I'm crazy about sushi'). 열정적이고 감정이 넘치는 뉘앙스.
expressions.example: 'Why do you always agree to help her even at midnight?' 'Because I'm crazy about her, okay?' → '왜 자정에도 그 여자 부탁을 들어줘?' '왜냐면 그 여자한테 완전히 빠져있거든, 알겠어?'

occ.speaker: Joshua
occ.text: Yeah, that would be fantastic! My-my nephew is crazy about the Knicks! This is fantastic, thank you so much Rachel. (He takes the tickets and leaves as Mr. Waltham returns.)

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: No I'm not okay. The only guy I've ever been crazy about has gone to Minsk and I may never...I may never see him again. (Crying.)

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Yeah well, I'm guessing after this she's not going to be crazy about electricity either.

expressions.expression: the minute you ~ (= as soon as you ~)
expressions.dialogue:
Monica: What is it with you people! I mean, the minute you start to feel something, you have to run away?
Chandler: I know, that's why I don't want to go tonight, I'm afraid I'm going to say something stupid.
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: 뭔가 느끼기 시작하자마자 도망가야 해? — 어떤 일이 시작되는 순간 곧바로 다른 일이 벌어지는 패턴을 강조
expressions.nuance: 'the minute (that) ~' = '~하는 순간, ~하자마자'. 'as soon as'보다 구어적이고 감정이 실린 표현. 특정 행동 패턴을 비판하거나 관찰할 때 자주 씀.
expressions.example: The minute things get serious, he disappears. I'm done with him. → 일이 진지해지는 순간 그는 사라져버려. 이제 그 사람이랑은 끝이야.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Well, it would be easier to move just right across the hall. Wait a minute, unless you're thinking about Naked Wednesday's.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Yeah, she's been out there for twenty minutes, I'm surprised you didn't hear her on the way over.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Okay, that's gonna take them a minute. Do you have anything else you wanna get off your chest?

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: I've scared ya'! I've said too much! I'm hopeless, and awkward, and desperate for love!!
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: ~가 너무너무 간절하다 / ~를 절실히 원하다
expressions.nuance: 비즈니스('desperate for a solution'), 감정('desperate for approval'), 일상('desperate for sleep') 등 폭넓게 사용. 여기서는 Chandler의 자기비하 유머로 쓰여 절박함을 코믹하게 표현. 강도가 세어 상황에 따라 다소 과장된 인상을 줄 수 있음.
expressions.example: After three hours of hiking with no water, I was desperate for a cold drink. → 물 없이 세 시간 하이킹 후에 차가운 음료가 너무 간절했어.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: No! No! No! No it's not! No it's not! Come on! Phoebe, ours is totally different! I mean we don't have the... (Looks desperately for something different.) We don't have the...that lamp! And-and that screen is y'know, on the other side.
